class Solution {
public:
    int findTheLongestBalancedSubstring(string s) {
        int n = s.length();
        for (int k = min(n/2, 25); k >=0; --k) {
            int len = 2*k;

                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!valid) continue;
                // check next k 1s
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + k + j] != '1') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (valid) return 2*k;
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    int findTheLongestBalancedSubstring(string s) {
        int n = s.size();
        for (int k = n/2; k >=0; --k) {
            int len = 2 * k;
            for (int i = 0; i <= n - len; ++i) {
                // check first k 0s
                bool valid = true;
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + j] != '0') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!valid) continue;
                // check next k 1s
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + k + j] != '1') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (valid) return len;
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    int findTheLongestBalancedSubstring(string s) {
        int n = s.length();
        for (int k = n/2; k >= 0; --k) {
            int len = 2 * k;
            for (int i = 0; i <= n - len; ++i) {
                bool valid = true;
                // Check first k zeros
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + j] != '0') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!valid) continue;
                // Check next k ones
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + k + j] != '1') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (valid) return len;
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int findTheLongestBalancedSubstring(string s) {
        int n = s.length();
        for (int k = n/2; k >= 0; --k) {
            int len = 2 * k;
            for (int i = 0; i <= n - len; ++i) {
                bool valid = true;
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + j] != '0') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!valid) continue;
                for (int j = 0; j < k; ++j) {
                    if (s[i + k + j] != '1') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (valid) return len;
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }
};
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^3)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution checks all possible substring lengths from largest to smallest, verifying if any substring meets the balanced condition (k zeros followed by k ones). It uses nested loops to check each candidate substring, which is efficient for small input sizes (n ≤ 50) but has cubic time complexity. The key strength is simplicity and correctness for constrained inputs, while the weakness is poor scalability for larger strings.
